title: Man in Carbonite

{{Quote|This finely rendered carbonite sculpture, the person of Han Solo? No. What I brought you today is ''art''. Art created by the Dark Lord that happened to ''use'' Han Solo as material, like another artist might shape clay.|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]|<ref name="Tales from Jabba's Palace">''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]''</ref>}}
'''''Man in Carbonite''''' was the name given to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] after the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] had been turned into a [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] sculpture by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Vader released the sculpture into the custody of Boba Fett, who sold it to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] for 250,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]—separately from the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Solo, which itself was 100,000 credits. ''Man in Carbonite'' was later destroyed when [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] freed Solo. The [[Skywalker Tour]] of Jabba the Hutt's former [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]] boasted an accurate replica of the infamous piece of art ''Man in Carbonite''.
